summary: An online electronics and watches store website called Gadgets Gear. The site will showcase and sell electronics and watches online.
architectural_notes: Store name: Gadgets Gear — online electronics and watches retailer
known_issues: Admin panel login credentials unclear to user — admin authentication requires clarification on username/password setup

existing_features.title: Create a homepage featuring a full-width hero banner with promotional messaging, a featured products section showcasing top electronics and watches, and a category section with clearly labeled cards for 'Electronics' and 'Watches' that link to their respective listing pages.

Build a product listing page for each category (Electronics and Watches) that displays products in a grid layout with filter options (e.g., price range, brand) and sorting controls (e.g., price low-to-high, newest).
Build a product detail page that shows a product image gallery, full description, price, stock status, and an 'Add to Cart' button.

Implement a persistent shopping cart that tracks added products and quantities, with a cart page showing item list, subtotal per item, order total, and a checkout flow with a form for shipping and payment details.

Create a global navigation header with the Gadgets Gear logo/wordmark, a search bar for searching products, a cart icon with item count badge, and navigation links to the Electronics and Watches category pages.

Apply a sleek, modern dark-themed visual design with a deep charcoal/near-black background, crisp white and light-gray typography, amber/gold accent color for highlights and CTAs, and a clean sans-serif typeface. Enforce this theme consistently across all pages and components.

Create a password-protected admin panel frontend page at route /admin. On first visit, show a login form with a password field. On successful authentication (verified against the backend verifyAdminPassword function), store the session in localStorage and display the full admin dashboard. On failed authentication, show an error message. Include a logout button that clears the session.

Build a product management table in the admin dashboard that lists all products with columns for name, category, brand, price, stock, and actions. Include an 'Add Product' button that opens a form/modal to create a new product with fields for name, category (Electronics or Watches), description, price, brand, imageUrl, and stock quantity. Each row should have Edit and Delete action buttons. The Edit button opens a pre-filled form/modal to update the product. The Delete button prompts a confirmation dialog before removing the product.
Add a link to the admin panel (/admin) in the site footer, labeled 'Admin' under a staff or store management section, so administrators can easily locate the panel.

Inspect the backend verifyAdminPassword function to confirm the hardcoded admin password, then update the Admin login page to remove any username field (since authentication is password-only) and ensure the login form clearly labels the single 'Password' input. Display a helper hint on the login page indicating there is no username — only a password is required.

new_feature_requests.summary: Homepage with featured products, hero banner, and categories (electronics & watches)
Product listing pages for electronics and watches with filters and sorting
Product detail page with images, description, price, and add-to-cart
Navigation header with logo, search bar, cart icon, and category links

Implement backend data storage for products with fields: id, name, category (electronics | watches), description, price, brand, imageUrl, and stock quantity. Expose query functions to list all products, list by category, and get a single product by id.

Pre-populate the backend with at least 8 sample products — a mix of electronics (e.g., headphones, smartphones, laptops) and watches (e.g., smartwatch, analog dress watch) — with realistic names, descriptions, prices, and placeholder image URLs.
Build a password-protected admin panel to add, edit, delete products and manage categories

Extend the backend actor to support full CRUD operations for products: add a new product, update an existing product's name, description, price, brand, imageUrl, stock quantity, and category, and delete a product by ID. Store products in a mutable HashMap rather than a static map so changes persist across calls. Add an admin password verification function that accepts a password string and returns a boolean.
